raw_input:
[5.0/5] Can't stop laughing!!!!! I would like to give thanks to Count Beefula for an outstanding and inspiring review of this product. [1.0/5] Really though? This is fake, right? All of these reviews have to be fake, or sarcastic. [5.0/5] The other lip balm Best gift idea ever! I got some for all my girlfriends and they raved about it! I did not get to try it myself however, as the dog stole mine and later there was a horrible mess to deal with.
Oh, and speaking of horrible messes, be more careful than I was- I got the stocking stuffers mixed up and it caused an acute scene on Christmas morning when little Jackson opened what he thought was lip balm in front of grandma- just before she opened hers.
We are celebrating the holidays in a foreign country next year. [1.0/5] This stuff taste awful. They said bubble gum? A friend of mine told me about this stuff and I just ordered it without reading the description. Yuck taste like wax. This product was as misleading as that other nasty jam, KY Jelly. You know that stuff isn't really jelly at all? It's something to grease your ball bearings with or something. But I didn't find out until I ruined what could have been a perfectly good peanut butter sandwich. Anyone have any other tastey treat ideas they would like to give me? [4.0/5] This kitty's got a new coat ... of paint! I wasn't even aware of my embarrassing genital discoloration before I stumbled across this product. I haven't been this "fresh" and (temporarily) pink for 25 years! (I'm 32, you do the math ...).
My boyfriend hasn't noticed the difference yet (he prefers to do it with the lights off, eyes closed), but one of these days I'm going to "accidentally" show him my sexy new vagina and prepare to get skewered!
Just think ... if it weren't for your product, many women might not even consider the color of their labia to be a problem at all. Can you imagine? Women walking around with gray, discolored vaginas in their pants and feeling totally OKAY about it? Gross! [5.0/5] Doesn't your man deserve perfection? Ladies, we all know that we are nothing unless we can catch a man and keep him. We also know that the fat, balding, underachieving, middle-aged shlub we married will ditch us in a moment for that man-stealing ho Miss Universe if we aren't superficially perfect at all times. But what to do when we start to look like one of those "normal" women instead of an always-perfect supermodel?

My New Pink Button is the answer! Formulated by a quack manicurist - sorry, I mean a "paramedical esthetician" - My New Pink Button will take off the years, take off the children, and take off the reality! Its patented "natural" "formula" (wink wink) is guaranteed to remove what other, lesser women call "normal vaginal coloration" and replace it with pink perfection! And all this with a minimum of permanent scarring and complete loss of sexual response - but who needs that anyway, sex is for men!

My New Pink Button has never been tested on animals (so if it burns your skin off or causes cancer that's your problem) and contains twenty disposable applicators (so you'll be able to fool him twenty times!).

Remember, girls: when your man comes home after a long, hard day of standing around the water cooler telling dirty jokes and ogling the secretaries, he deserves the hottest, most ultra-conformist woman he can find. You owe it to him to be as superficially perfect as you can! Buy My New Pink Button today! [1.0/5] Why? I tell my children not to rub mystery substances on their genitals. I would hope that most intelligent human beings would follow suit.

If you get to a point in your life that you feel it's necessary to change the color of your labia in order to feel good about yourself, you should perhaps re-examine your priorities. [5.0/5] if the underwear fits! uhm.can i use this on my ex boyfriend's junk? i figure since he IS a p*#sie,he may as well look like one,too.
please reply soon.i am working against the clock and only have a small window of opportunity to help me bc he is
passed out drunk.
thanks, in advance! [1.0/5] On behalf of adult film stars everywhere - We are angry about this product! What was once reserved for the elite echelons of adult film is now available for the general public?

May I be the first to say this is an outrage! MNPB is an industry leading special effect makeup created by our top labia color and design experts. Our unions payed billions of dollars to perfect the day glow pink hue you see here. Clearly some scab has gone and leaked the secret formula and supplied this precious gem to all you prudish peasants.

Where this traitor found a reliable supply of blue-ringed octopi venom is beyond me. (The most natural of natural ingredients!)

Let me tell all you peasants out there that just because this is publicly available now does not mean we wont always be pioneers in labia special effects, just now we are working on a new secret formula that is the perfect shade of tangerine which we are sure will make your husbands and boyfriends reject your blinding pink beef curtains and come calling for stage lighting on lovely tangerine flesh. Because remember ladies, no man wants you to look natural, if everything about yourself isn't a lie, you will never find true love. [5.0/5] MNPB Gave My Vagina A New life! I'd been using MNPB for months and let me tell ya, my sex life was NEVER better!

Each night I would slap some of this snatch staining salve on and hit the club. While the rest of my bloated lush body got sweaty and lackluster throwing back drinks and grinding up against a cloud of Axe body sprayed men to the latest MyCy jam, my vajay stayed as pink and fresh (from a distance) as ever. Each night I'd take at least one new guy home with me, and dude, the free drinks did. not. stop. That was the most fun 9-months of my life!

Only complaint: I could never tell if I was on my period or not, and turns out I was pregnant the whole time. Bummer. But don't worry betches, this story has a happy ending... little Baby Gaga was delivered in the club's bathroom by a super muscular bouncer (and, yes, my new common law husband) Brad-the-Dad. Can you believe that was already his nickname?!? Kinda perfect.... [1.0/5] Kind of an acidic, astringent, bubble-gum flavor. Looks great, but I could not say the same for the taste. My husband was so very disappointed. It's back to the drawing board. [2.0/5] Glows in the dark? I put this on my button and turned the lights off, and my boyfriend still couldn't see it. I thought this was supposed to make it easier for him to find it in the dark? It would be nice if it came in some other colors. Please make a glow in the dark version. The Techno-Glow body paint gives me a UTI. [3.0/5] pretty good, fast shipping Arrived on time, came with an extra sampler pack, which was nice. I had to call customer service to change the shipping address but they were very helpful.

My only issue was the size of the portion. Valentine's day was on friday, and by the next week's tuesday my girlfriend had already run out. And the color of her crotch hadn't really changed that much. I tried some myself and noticed some change but I was hoping for more. I wish they sold bigger sizes or maybe bulk packs like at Costco, because I think my girlfriend needed a few more applications to really start looking good again.

The cream goes on smooth but its pretty hard to get off fingers or most clothes. Khakis were the worst probably, couldn't get the drips out until we tried some selzer water. However no problem with cotton socks! If it gets on your skin, peanut butter actually works better than soap to remove it. Someday the company needs to start making a travel size or a cleaner scrub, because she was always spilling it, which really gets on your nerves when its such an expensive item.

I would recommend this to most people but I've had somewhat better luck with other types of dye. This one seems to work more on the skin than the pubes. The directions were sort of confusing but if you contact CS they can pdf you a clearer version. They usually have a good deal on rush shipping, just remember to ask first, same with re-orders, you get 10% off but you need to request the discount code. [1.0/5] Offensive Really? Now we're supposed to be dying our coochies in chemicals to be sexier? On top of Brazilian waxes, we now have another useless and probably painful (and probably unsafe) step to take in order to get us closer to our pre-pubescent forms. How totally offensive, absurd, and disgusting. [1.0/5] It's paint. Originally posted by 'Me'
"I tell my children not to rub mystery substances on their genitals. I would hope that most intelligent human beings would follow suit."

Very good advice.
If anyone really wants a home remedy to make their labia smaller and redder, then poultry shears are the way to go.

And why's there no video review ?

response:
Product reviews for pink lady part dye thing. Mixed feelings. Caveman summarize now:

Many humans find product funny. Some make jokes. Count Beefula get praise for funny review.

Some humans angry reviews fake and sarcastic. Fair point.

Woman buy for girlfriends as gift. Dog steal product. Big mess. Also mix up with lip balm at Christmas. Child open in front grandma. Bad scene.

Man order thinking food. Taste like wax. Ruin peanut butter sandwich. Very angry. Call product KY Jelly. Not food. Man not know this before order.

Woman use on cat parts. Boyfriend not notice. Woman worry about accidental exposure. Think other women should consider product for gray parts problem.

Sarcastic woman say product good for keeping man. Mock idea that woman need pink parts to satisfy man. Call creator fake doctor. Warn about scarring and lost feeling. Say tested on animals. Very angry tone.

Human say not tell children rub mystery on genitals. Hope others smart enough agree. Say examine priorities if feel need change color of parts.

Person want use on ex boyfriend parts. Drunk. Running out of time.

Adult film person very angry product now available to regular humans. Say formula stolen from industry. Claim blue ringed octopus venom inside. Very upset.

Woman use product go clubbing. Meet man. Have baby in club bathroom. Bouncer become husband. Name Brad. Happy ending sort of.

Husband disappointed product taste bad. Back to drawing board.

Product glow in dark apparently. Give UTI. Boyfriend think easier to find in dark.

Person order for Valentine Day. Shipping okay. Product hard to apply. Stain khakis bad. Peanut butter remove from skin better than soap. Directions confusing. Suggest sell at Costco.

Human say product offensive. Compare to Brazilian wax. Call unsafe and disgusting. Angry about chemicals on parts.

Final human suggest poultry shears as home remedy instead. Also make video review. Very not recommend product.
